title: helianthemum guttatum var. macrosepalum
intro: Helianthemum guttatum var. macrosepalum, also known as Rockrose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Cistaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Herbal preparations of Helianthemum guttatum var. macrosepalum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to reap its health benefits.
